## [03:05 - 06:08]

sessions and your curriculum and your amazing coaching to get them the rest of the way. So your job is to help them to understand better how to get where they want to go. One way that's really helped me to understand how does coaching really help is coaching is kind of like selling after the sale. So think of it like this. The sale is that moment of commitment where someone is agreeing to pay the money, they're agreeing to set aside the time to join you for coaching. And we'll talk later in the course about the sales process. But once they're sold on your coaching program, coaching itself is kind of like selling your client over and over and over and over again on working towards their goal. ## [06:08 - 08:11]

on the journey that you weren't really anticipating. And that's the value of having the coach is to sell you on, hey, maybe we can try another route, maybe we can do something different than you originally anticipated because let's face it, if it's really a coachable goal that was worth investing in in the first place, they probably need that extra support to be able to get there. And so I think of it personally as I'm basically there to help sell on, okay, why are we working towards this goal again? What is it that we've already invested in again? Are you still invested in the goal? How invested are we in the goal? Okay, well, what have we already tried? What are we wanting to try again? Helping my clients stay in alignment with their best character, their best version of themselves, the resources that they have to pull on.
